vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow

triage_decision.reason: Changing the permissions of /etc/gshadow is a standard STIG hardening step that does not affect system boot, networking, or critical services, and the fix is well‑defined (chmod command). The change is low‑risk and reversible.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Improper permissions could expose group password hashes to unprivileged users; Incorrect permissions may allow unauthorized modifications to group shadow entries
